one_line_description: Visual discovery platform whose API v5 covers pins, boards, ads, catalogs, and analytics.
auth_summary: API v5 uses OAuth2: authorization code flow (with refresh tokens) for user-context access to the full API, plus client credentials grant for app-own-behalf/machine-to-machine cases. No plain API key.
access_notes: Register an app on developers.pinterest.com, then request Trial Access (limited rate limits); full-scope Standard/production access requires an approval process including demonstrating a working interactive OAuth login flow to Pinterest.
api_surface_summary: REST API v5 covering pins, boards, user account data, ads (campaigns/ad groups/ads/reporting), audiences, catalogs/shopping, and conversions API.
main_blocker: Trial access has limited rate limits and standard access requires Pinterest's review/approval process
agent_notes: No official Pinterest MCP found — only community servers (some, like terryso/mcp-pinterest, scrape rather than use the API; collactivelabs' uses the real API). Pinterest maintains an official api-quickstart repo on GitHub. Trial-vs-standard access details came from official docs surfaced in search; I did not fetch the access-request page itself.
